FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Morgan Stanley clears digital-asset trust hurdle while cannabis banking gains legislative momentum; regional M&A and CRA compliance updates round out the day.

bullets.text: Morgan Stanley wins conditional OCC approval for digital-asset trust charter, subject to $50M tier 1 capital maintenance and regulator sign-off on material business changes.
Sen. Merkley reintroduces cannabis banking reform legislation, riding recent federal rescheduling of medical cannabis to unlock banking access for the sector.
Fed and agencies update CRA-triggering list of distressed/underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geographies, creating lending incentives for regional banks.
Commerce Bancorp ($37.5B assets) acquiring Nolan & Associates—its second deal in 12 months, signaling continued regional consolidation in wealth/boutique advisory.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: RIA consolidation accelerates with EP Wealth's $900M acquisition, while Hightower navigates leadership turnover amid a Goldman-led sale process and DOL rules threaten public markets.

bullets.text: EP Wealth buys Opes Wealth Management for $900M in Silicon Valley RIA play, signaling continued growth-stage consolidation after EP's employee incentive program announcement.
Hightower president Roberto Stewart exits mid-sale process as Goldman Sachs-led buyer hunt continues; RIA names three new senior hires (Phillips, Bullard, Ahluwahlia) to manage transition.
Rep. Maxine Waters opposes DOL safe harbor rule for alternatives in retirement accounts, warning it could trigger exodus from public markets and harm retirees.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Insurance market showing mixed signals: cyber loss ratios deteriorating while nat cat capacity improves and TRIA gets extended.

bullets.text: US cyber insurance loss ratio hit 53% in 2025, up for second straight year—margin compression accelerating as third-party claims surge.
Kin's reciprocals scored $1.9B nat cat reinsurance at 25% cost reduction per dollar of risk at June 1 renewal, signaling softer market.
House passes HR 7128 extending TRIA terrorism backstop through 2034 with bipartisan support, locking in federal indemnification for catastrophic claims.
Lemonade trimmed quota share cession to 18% from 20% at July 1 renewal while expanding cat protection—betting on retaining more underwriting risk.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Challenger banking faces distress while payments infrastructure modernizes via stablecoins, agents, and blockchain settlement.

bullets.text: Atom Bank's £600M sale process is collapsing due to lack of buyer interest, exposing broader weakness in the digital challenger segment.
Visa, Mastercard, US Bank, Google, and Coinbase are backing Open USD stablecoin—major sign of mainstream payments moving to blockchain rails.
EIB's first DLT-native commercial paper issuance on Clearstream signals institutional capital markets infrastructure is moving onto blockchain.
Private debt secondaries growth claims face skepticism as non-listed BDCs cool credit fundraising momentum.

PE & Deals.lede: PE performance concerns dominate: $75bn Colorado PERA cuts exposure amid underperformance, while Generation IM raises $1bn on longer-hold thesis and ILPA tightens GP economics scrutiny.

bullets.text: Colorado PERA ($75bn) weighing PE pullback after benchmark underperformance—a red flag for LP appetite amid broader return skepticism.
Generation IM closes $1bn fund betting longer hold periods unlock PE transformation; challenges standard 5-7 year exit timelines as constraint.
ILPA updates carried interest valuation guidance as secondaries market evolves and LP scrutiny of GP economics intensifies—tightening the screws on fee structures.
Arizona public pension tilting PE toward distressed software (debt maturity walls, valuation compression from peak multiples)—opportunistic positioning in dislocated segment.

Markets & Macro.lede: Regulatory uncertainty from SCOTUS ruling clouds deal timelines; wealth advisory consolidation accelerates; crypto volatility persists despite ETF growth; systemic risks from AI trading and unregulated products draw regulatory scrutiny.

bullets.text: Supreme Court expanded presidential power over federal regulators while preserving Fed independence, creating deal approval uncertainty across financial services—major headwind for M&A timelines.
Schroders selling Benchmark Capital (financial planning) to Swedish PE for £200m+ signals continued asset manager retreat from non-core advisory as wealth consolidation deepens.
Bitcoin ETFs pulled in $14B YTD inflows but haven't dampened crypto volatility—institutional adoption theory failing the test during market stress.
BoE warns AI-powered trading could trigger systemic volatility through automated herding; regulators eyeing 'kill switches' to prevent market disruption.

Newsletters.lede: Structured products surge while crypto bleeds; Morgan Stanley eyes Dallas expansion amid lukewarm client demand for alternatives.

bullets.text: Autocallable ETFs hit $2.5B AUM in first year by bringing structured products to retail—a meaningful democratization of previously institutional-only territory.
Bitcoin ETF outflows hit record highs as crypto winter deepens; question mark on whether this is sustained downturn or contrarian entry point.
Morgan Stanley committing $1.3B to Dallas ops with $18.5M in municipal incentives—significant footprint play in competitive wealth hub consolidation.
Wealth advisors report client appetite for alternative investments remains tepid despite broader private market access expansion—distribution challenge persists.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: AI infrastructure hitting real constraints: token budgets are breaking enterprise workflows, unit economics aren't saving money despite price drops, and academic integrity is eroding fast.

bullets.text: Factory ($1.5B, Sequoia-backed) surfacing the token budget crisis—AI agents are maxing out on context window costs and size limits, forcing architects to rethink autonomous workflow design.
Despite 600x drop in token costs over six years, enterprise AI spending is accelerating *faster* than unit-cost declines, creating a classic adoption curve where demand outpaces commoditization.
Brown econ prof caught widespread LLM cheating in ECON 1170, signaling academic integrity erosion at elite institutions as models become invisible testing tools.
Decentralized prediction markets (Risk Labs) already gamed by sophisticated bettors despite elaborate crypto/voting safeguards—Web3 oracle design meeting real-world exploitability.

all.lede: Structured products democratize via ETFs while challenger banking struggles; Morgan Stanley clears crypto custody hurdle as wealth consolidation accelerates.

bullets.text: Autocallable ETFs hit $2.5B in AUM in their first year by bringing institutional-quality structured products to retail—a potential growth vector for FIG platforms.
Atom Bank's sale collapses below £600M valuation target, signaling distress in UK challenger banking and potential M&A opportunities in digital bank segment.
Morgan Stanley clears OCC conditional approval for digital-asset trust charter with $50M capital floor—strategic win in institutional crypto custody.
EP Wealth's $900M Silicon Valley RIA acquisition reflects ongoing wealth management consolidation; DOL alts rule debate heats up as Dem lawmaker warns of public market exodus.
